errors.message: storageEngine is set at metricsPlan level; contract requires postgresTimescaleDB for every metric table (already true), but plan-level field may be ignored/unsupported and creates ambiguity about enforcement.
todaySalesMetrics is sourced from Order and OrderItem but lacks order_id dimension (direct relationship OrderItem -> Order).

todaySalesMetrics includes MenuItem/MenuCategory dimensions implying joins, but sourceEntities omit MenuItem/MenuCategory; if they are direct relationships of OrderItem they should be declared as sourceEntities and FK dims should reflect ontology consistently.

topSellingItemsMetrics sources include MenuItem but lacks direct FK dims for MenuItem's direct ontology relationships (e.g., menu_category_id if MenuItem -> MenuCategory is direct). Dimension exists, but ensure it's FK from MenuItem not derived; ontology relationship not stated; ambiguity is a contract-level miss for T-013.

lowStockMetrics sourced from InventoryTransaction and InventoryBalanceSnapshot but missing direct FK dims such as inventory_transaction_id / inventory_balance_snapshot_id (if modeled as entities) and lacks clear FK dims for InventoryTransaction -> InventoryItem (only inventoryItemId present, ok) and InventoryTransaction -> DailyShift (not present if exists). T-013 requires FKs of source entities’ direct relationships; relationships not fully covered.

shiftSummaryMetrics uses source entities DailyShift, Order, OrderItem but includes menuItemId and diningTableId while omitting menuCategoryId (if MenuItem -> MenuCategory direct) and uses orderId type text inconsistent with other UUID FKs; also missing orderType dimension if Order has it as direct attribute used in dashboards.
dashboardPages[0].actor uses field 'actor' not 'actorId'; contract says dashboards must target a final plan actorId.

dashboard sources reference metricTableIds; OK, but widgets' metricId fields are measureIds without qualification; if schema expects fully qualified IDs this can break. Ensure consistent referencing contract.

warnings.message: Several sourceBaseTables are flagged unknown (order_item, daily_shift, inventory_balance_snapshot, inventory_transaction). Ensure they match moduleTableIds: order, diningTable, dailyShift, inventoryTransaction, inventoryBalanceSnapshot.
Base tables use snake_case but moduleTableIds include camelCase for some (dailyShift etc.). Consider aligning sourceBaseTables to approved module table ids or define mapping clearly.
shiftSummaryMetrics includes menuItemId and orderId dimensions which may explode cardinality for a 'summary' table; consider separate fact tables vs summary grain.

questions: Quais são as relações diretas (FKs) na ontologia entre: OrderItem->MenuItem, MenuItem->MenuCategory, Order->DailyShift, InventoryTransaction->DailyShift/Order/InventoryItem, InventoryBalanceSnapshot->DailyShift/InventoryItem? (para validar T-013)
Os IDs de base table devem usar moduleTableIds (order, dailyShift, inventoryTransaction...) ou nomes físicos snake_case? Qual é o contrato esperado para sourceBaseTables?
O schema de dashboard widget.metricId referencia measureId simples ou exige qualificação (ex.: todaySalesMetrics.totalRevenue)?
trace: checked contract focus items: metric count vs approved artifacts (4 ok); storageEngine per table (all postgresTimescaleDB ok); updateResponsibility layer_3_usecases (plan-level ok); dashboard references metric tables in index (ok); validated actorId field and T-013 dimensions coverage; incorporated deterministic warnings without contradiction.
